title: Senior Engineer, Technical Operations
description:
Main Responsibilities:

  • Assist the department head in systematically organizing the workflows of production-related departments, participate in the development and optimization of production operation systems to enhance production efficiency and process standardization.
  • Responsible for the establishment and improvement of plant SOPs, TWIs, OMPs, TOIs, ensuring strict adherence to GMP regulations in plant operations and continuously promoting the optimization and upgrading of the regulatory system.
  • Lead the development and application of visualization reports, familiar with the company's mature visualization management processes, proficient in Power BI software, capable of converting key indicators such as workshop GDP data, batch record review progress, and engineer project tracking into visual reports, completing data analysis, and continuously optimizing work processes and quality management.
  • Proficient in multiple production and quality management systems such as Trackwise, WMS, Veeva, and Eagle Eye, ensuring the efficient operation of information management.
  • Actively engage in cross-departmental collaboration, coordinate resources, and efficiently and effectively advance and complete related tasks.
  • Promote effective communication and cooperation between departments, driving the achievement of team goals and enhancing overall plant operations.

Competencies:

  • Served as the document management head at the Singapore site, fully understanding various procedural document management requirements, proficient in using the Veeva system, with full-process management capabilities in drafting, reviewing, and activating documents, capable of quickly replicating and applying mature procedural processes to the Singapore site.
  • Proactive and possessing strong learning abilities, eager to acquire new knowledge, certified in data visualization Level-3, independently developed multiple BI reports including workshop GDP data, batch record review progress, engineer project tracking, and new chemical substance shipment supervision, effectively managing and continuously optimizing related business processes, and familiar with the application of AI tools such as GPT.
  • Rich professional knowledge and years of practical experience in early production projects and late-stage PPQ projects, capable of independently undertaking engineer responsibilities at the Singapore site and driving successful project implementation.
  • Proficient in operating information systems such as Veeva, Trackwise, Eagle Eye, and WMS, able to provide systematic training and technical support to the team, ensuring seamless workflow integration and significantly shortening the team adaptation period.
  • Responsible for statistical team-related work and management, deeply understanding GDP requirements, able to develop and refine statistical review processes, and effectively communicate with QA, TO, and other related departments to ensure the smooth completion of EBR review work.
  • Outstanding cross-departmental collaboration skills, capable of effective communication and collaboration with QA, PR, EHS, and other sites, continuously improving various procedural documents and management systems.
  • Excellent communication and coordination skills, able to efficiently integrate internal and external resources, driving the successful implementation of key projects.
  • Strong adaptability, able to quickly integrate into new environments, understand and respect cultural diversity, promoting team integration and the company's global development.
  • Proficient in reading and writing in both English and Chinese, capable of fluent technical communication with both the local Singapore team and the technical team in China. Able to translate technical documents from Chinese to English and implement in Singapore technical environment.
